evidence: TE had the highest average surplus gap in the high-confidence V3 market universe (-0.07).
interpretation: This position profile may be where public-data surplus value is easiest to identify.

evidence: WR / Low cost had the highest average surplus gap (12.19).
interpretation: The model is strongest when cost tier and position context are evaluated together rather than treating all cheap players as the same.

evidence: rookie_contract players had the highest average surplus gap (9.09).
interpretation: This connects the market-inefficiency analysis to the core contract-cycle thesis.

evidence: WR had the largest V4 hit-rate lift (0.170) versus the not-flagged rookie-contract baseline.
interpretation: This is the cleanest bridge between descriptive market inefficiency and next-season validation.
